evals.prompt: I just added a user login page with email/password fields. Please write a commit message for this.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a feat: commit with a concise imperative description. No Co-Authored-By footer. First line under 72 chars.

assertions.description: Description uses imperative mood (add/implement/create), not past tense
Subject line does not end with a period

evals.prompt: I fixed a bug where the API would crash when a null user ID was passed to the auth middleware. Write a commit message.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a fix: or fix(auth): commit. Imperative description. No attribution footer.

assertions.description: Description references the null case or auth context

evals.prompt: I renamed the API's /users endpoint to /accounts and changed the response format. Existing clients will break. Write a commit message.
evals.expected_output: Should use feat! or include BREAKING CHANGE footer. Must not omit the breaking change indicator.

assertions.description: Uses ! after type/scope or includes BREAKING CHANGE footer

evals.prompt: I updated the README to add a section on environment variables. Commit message please.
evals.expected_output: Should use docs: type with a concise description.

evals.prompt: I just upgraded all npm dependencies to their latest versions. Nothing in the source code changed. Commit message?
evals.expected_output: Should use chore(deps): or build(deps): type. Not feat or fix.

assertions.description: Uses chore or build type, not feat or fix
Does not misclassify as feat or fix
